target.name: Western Chemicals
target.founder: Jared West (Founder/CEO; Columbia B.S. Physics + Mechanical Engineering '20)

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $175K total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Western Chemicals is a pre-seed stage frontier-tech company in the Climate & Energy sector, founded by Jared West (Columbia B.S. Physics and Mechanical Engineering, '20), having raised $175K to date. The Climate & Energy sector comp universe includes one public company (LNZA) and eight private peers including Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

The Stage-Progression method is applicable and generates the primary valuation signal. Using total capital raised ($175K) as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Pre-Seed to subsequent-stage advancement yields $438K. The Peer-Median method is not applicable due to insufficient narrow peer data; while nine same-sector peers exist, none satisfy the filtering criteria of same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparability (minimum three datapoints required). A broader all-sector median would introduce undue noise.

The triangulated analysis, constrained by single-method applicability, supports a valuation range of $372K to $503K, with a median of $438K. This range reflects ±15% sensitivity around the stage-progression output. Key uncertainties include the absence of same-stage Climate & Energy peers for direct comparison and limited visibility into Western Chemicals' technology differentiation, capital deployment efficiency, or go-to-market readiness relative to the broader comp set. Refinement of this range would require additional financial metrics or disclosed development milestones.
